translated: ProSeka AfterTalk: Reaching Out for a Tangled Tomorrow, yay!
Good evening, everyone.
I'm Hinata Sato, the voice of Mizuki Akiyama from Nightcord at 25:00.
This ProSeka AfterTalk is a stream where we look back on the event while reading the messages you've sent in.
This time, we're focusing on the "Reaching Out for a Tangled Tomorrow" event.
I hope you all enjoyed the story.
The event actually just ended yesterday. How was it for all of you?
Did you like it?
I really hope you all had a good time with it.
It was a story about the members of Niigo facing their own futures
and thinking about what they want to do later in life.
I think it might have really hit home for those of you who are at that stage of deciding on your education or career path.
Starting in April, AfterTalk got a bit of a renewal, and we've been collecting messages in advance via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
Thank you so much to everyone who sent one in.
I'll be reading some of your messages during the show, so look forward to that.
Also, if you want to share your thoughts on the event or this stream, please post using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat, and I'll check the hashtag later too,
so I'll try to pick out any comments that catch my eye.
There are so many overseas comments! I can barely find the Japanese ones. People are saying the ribbon is cute, or that my hair matches Mizuki's...
The stream is probably a bit delayed, so people are just now noticing my hair and outfit.
I always try to do this, but I really made an effort to match the event illustration today.
I came here with the illustration in mind.
Good evening!
Good evening, and thank you all for joining.
Oh, someone said, "The story and the new song were so good, I sobbed."
I'll talk more about the song in a bit.
When I saw it at home, I was like, "What is this?!"
I mean, "What even is this?!"
I really felt the incredible effort the team put into it.
Right, right.
I'll save that talk for later.
Now then, let's dive right into the story for "Reaching Out for a Tangled Tomorrow."
We've received messages about this story as well,
so I'd like to look back on the plot while reading through them.
First, we have a message from Kotori no Mama-san.
"Hello, Hina-san! Konnigo!"
Konnigo!
"My child and I both enjoy playing ProSeka together."
"The event featuring my favorite, Mizuki-chan, was incredibly cute, funny, and moving."
"I'm so glad we got to see Mizuki-chan's various feelings through her past experiences and her interactions with those around her."
"I think it's very difficult to stay true to yourself and keep doing what you love without getting lost or hitting a wall."
"But I believe that if you hold onto those feelings, the day will come when they are rewarded."
"Just like the members of Niigo, I'm sure there are people out there who will see you for who you are"
"and accept you."
"I truly love the stories in ProSeka."
"Hina-san, thank you so much for always voicing her."
"I'll continue to support both Mizuki-chan and Hina-san with all my heart!"
Thank you so much, Kotori no Mama-san!
In this story, Mizuki's sister comes home from overseas to run a pop-up shop. I felt it was a huge step forward for Mizuki to want the other Niigo members to meet her.
Kanade and Mafuyu had met Yuuki-san before, but Ena hadn't.
Seeing them finally meet was one of the ways the story progressed.
Mizuki loves so many things, like making videos,
remaking clothes, visiting boutiques, and looking at makeup.
I think it's amazing that they're already facing the question of what they want to do for a career at this stage.
When I was looking at colleges, I was just visiting open campuses thinking, "If I'm going to pay for this, I need to study something that leaves me with marketable skills."
So I felt like my approach to finding what I liked was completely different from Mizuki's.
Even so, I think it was a very important time for Mizuki to re-examine what they love at their own pace.
It's a big deal to realize that your passion for clothes isn't just a hobby, but something you might want as a career. I was just so happy to see Mizuki smiling.
